overview: Izzet Control in Pauper relies on countering key spells and generating card advantage to outlast opponents. By leveraging cheap interaction and mana disruption, the deck stalls aggressive strategies while racing other control shells. The goal is to stabilize early and close out games with superior resource management.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage
Countering decisive threats to stall for value
Closing out games with late-game threats or combat damage

keep_criteria: Hands with 2-3 lands and a counterspell
Hands featuring Force Spike for early disruption
Hands containing Lorien Revealed for mid-game refills
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one early interaction (Force Spike, Counterspell), and Lorien Revealed or a cantrip.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or mana
Mulligan flood/draw, hands with only high-cost spells, or hands lacking early disruption against aggro.

early_game: Play lands consistently to avoid mana screws
Hold up Counterspell or Force Spike for opponent's key plays
Avoid developing board unless opponent lacks interaction
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Force Spike on a 1-drop threat if possible.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Counterspell on a key spell or play a cantrip.
Sequence lands to ensure U/R availability
prioritize untapping lands before casting spells to maximize interaction windows.
mid_game: Cast Lorien Revealed to refill hand and find answers
Trade spells efficiently to maintain resource advantage
Apply pressure only when opponent's interaction is exhausted
Hold 1-2 mana for interaction until Lorien Revealed is on the board.
Prioritize drawing into Lorien over overcommitting to board presence.
Use cantrips to cycle dead cards and maintain hand size.
Commit Lorien Revealed only after stabilizing against early aggression.
Hold back on casting non-essential spells if the opponent has sweepers or removal in hand.
Use Force Spike to protect Lorien from targeted removal.
late_game: Stall with counterspells until opponent runs out of resources
Cast remaining threats to close out the game
Prioritize countering win conditions over removal
Lorien Revealed generating Saprolings, combined with direct damage spells and card draw.
Win by out-valuing the opponent, sacrificing Lorien for damage/card advantage when threatened, and closing with combat or direct spells.
Go all-in when the opponent has no sweepers, you have card advantage, and Lorien is on the board.
Commit to attacking with Saprolings and casting remaining interaction to close the game.

key_cards.name: Lorien Revealed
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have a full hand or need to dig for answers; synergizes with counterspell-heavy hands to maintain resource parity.

key_cards.name: Counterspell
key_cards.notes: Hold up for opponent's threats or win conditions; use proactively to disrupt combos and force inefficient trades.

key_cards.name: Force Spike
key_cards.notes: Play on opponent's turn to counter spells while taxing their mana; excellent against aggressive decks and mana-intensive strategies.

sequencing_tips: Hold up Counterspell before casting Lorien Revealed to protect your advantage
Use Force Spike on opponent's turn to maximize mana denial
Prioritize countering threats over removal to preserve resources
Lorien Revealed is legendary, so you cannot have multiple copies
Watch for 'Legend rule' interactions if an opponent copies it.
Format legality heavily influences card choices, so verify your list's validity.
Avoid overextending into sweepers, and always hold up interaction for key threats before committing to Lorien.

matchups.advice: Prioritize Force Spike on their turn to counter early threats and disrupt mana.
Race for card advantage using Lorien Revealed and hold counterspells for their finishers.
Identify and counter the key combo pieces immediately; use Force Spike to disrupt mana-intensive turns.
Use cantrips to out-value counters, sacrifice Lorien for card advantage, and avoid overcommitting to expensive spells. Target their counterspells with spot removal or discard if available.
Apply early pressure with Force Spike and Counterspell to disrupt mana development. Bring in artifact hate and sweepers to neutralize late-game threats.

Win on card advantage and efficient interaction. Prioritize casting Lorien Revealed first to establish a board presence. Use Force Spike to protect your Lorien and disrupt theirs. Cycle through your deck to find the next key spell.

common_threats: Watch for opponent's mana development and discard patterns.
Key tells: holding up 3+ mana suggests a sweeper or combo piece
discarding draw spells indicates a control/combo plan
playing multiple lands without spells suggests ramp or combo.
